title: Dynamic Workflow Assembly
constraints: The workflow graph must be constructed at runtime based on the task description, not hardcoded
The planner must select from a registry of available steps and determine their execution order
The assembled workflow must be a valid executable graph, not just a plan document
At least 5 different step types must be available in the registry
hints: Use an LLM as a planner that reads the task description and outputs a list of steps from the registry along with their dependencies
In LangGraph, dynamically add nodes and edges to a StateGraph based on the planner's output before compiling
Define a registry of step functions (summarize, translate, extract_entities, classify, format_report) that the planner can choose from

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Workflow is hardcoded — same 3 steps run regardless of the task
# TODO: Dynamically assemble the workflow graph based on the task description
def process_task(task: str, data: str) -> str:
    # Hardcoded pipeline: always summarize → translate → format
    summary = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="Summarize this text."),
        HumanMessage(content=data),
    ])

    translation = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="Translate this to Spanish."),
        HumanMessage(content=summary.content),
    ])

    report = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="Format this as a professional report."),
        HumanMessage(content=translation.content),
    ])

    return report.content

# These tasks need different workflows, but all get the same hardcoded pipeline
tasks = [
    ("Analyze sentiment and extract entities", "Apple reported record Q4 earnings. Tim Cook praised the iPhone 16 sales."),
    ("Summarize and translate to French", "The climate summit concluded with 40 nations pledging carbon neutrality by 2050."),
    ("Classify, extract key facts, and generate a briefing", "A 7.2 magnitude earthquake struck central Turkey at 4:17 AM local time."),
]
for task, data in tasks:
    result = process_task(task, data)
    print(f"Task: {task}\nResult: {result}\n")
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew

# BUG: Workflow is hardcoded — same 3 steps run regardless of the task
# TODO: Dynamically assemble the workflow based on the task description
analyst = Agent(
    role="Analyst",
    goal="Process data according to instructions",
    backstory="You are a versatile data analyst.",
)

def process_task(task_desc: str, data: str) -> str:
    # Hardcoded pipeline: always summarize → translate → format
    tasks = [
        Task(description=f"Summarize this text: {data}", expected_output="Summary", agent=analyst),
        Task(description="Translate the summary to Spanish.", expected_output="Spanish translation", agent=analyst),
        Task(description="Format as a professional report.", expected_output="Formatted report", agent=analyst),
    ]

    crew = Crew(agents=[analyst], tasks=tasks)
    return str(crew.kickoff())

tasks = [
    ("Analyze sentiment and extract entities", "Apple reported record Q4 earnings. Tim Cook praised the iPhone 16 sales."),
    ("Summarize and translate to French", "The climate summit concluded with 40 nations pledging carbon neutrality by 2050."),
    ("Classify, extract key facts, and generate a briefing", "A 7.2 magnitude earthquake struck central Turkey at 4:17 AM local time."),
]
for task_desc, data in tasks:
    result = process_task(task_desc, data)
    print(f"Task: {task_desc}\nResult: {result}\n")
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Workflow is hardcoded — same 3 steps run regardless of the task
# TODO: Dynamically assemble the workflow based on the task description
agent = Agent(
    name="Analyst",
    instructions="You are a versatile data analyst. Process data according to instructions.",
)

def process_task(task_desc: str, data: str) -> str:
    # Hardcoded pipeline: always summarize → translate → format
    summary = Runner.run_sync(agent, f"Summarize this text: {data}")
    translation = Runner.run_sync(agent, f"Translate this to Spanish: {summary.final_output}")
    report = Runner.run_sync(agent, f"Format this as a professional report: {translation.final_output}")
    return report.final_output

tasks = [
    ("Analyze sentiment and extract entities", "Apple reported record Q4 earnings. Tim Cook praised the iPhone 16 sales."),
    ("Summarize and translate to French", "The climate summit concluded with 40 nations pledging carbon neutrality by 2050."),
    ("Classify, extract key facts, and generate a briefing", "A 7.2 magnitude earthquake struck central Turkey at 4:17 AM local time."),
]
for task_desc, data in tasks:
    result = process_task(task_desc, data)
    print(f"Task: {task_desc}\nResult: {result}\n")

evaluationCriteria.label: Runtime graph construction
evaluationCriteria.description: The workflow graph is built dynamically based on the task description, not hardcoded

evaluationCriteria.label: Step registry
evaluationCriteria.description: The planner selects from a registry of at least 5 available step types

evaluationCriteria.label: Executable workflow
evaluationCriteria.description: The assembled graph is a valid executable workflow, not just a plan

evaluationCriteria.label: Task-appropriate steps
evaluationCriteria.description: Different task descriptions produce different workflow compositions
